curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 14 units, 14 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, and uncertainty in measurement.
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory to calculate atomic/molecular masses.
Perform stoichiometric calculations using mole concept and percentage composition.
Trace the discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution from Bohr's model to Quantum Mechanical Model.
Understand the genesis of periodic classification and modern periodic law.
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, VSEPR theory, Valence Bond Theory, and Molecular Orbital Theory.
Apply concepts of calorimetry, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium conditions.
Analyze homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, ionic equilibrium, buffer solutions, and solubility equilibria.
Classify redox reactions in terms of electron transfer and electrode processes.
Apply nomenclature rules, identify isomerism, and analyze fundamental concepts of organic reaction mechanisms.
Differentiate between alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons.

topics: Importance of Chemistry and Nature of Matter
Properties of Matter and their Measurement
Atomic Models and Developments Leading to Bohr's Model

Study pH change in titration of a strong base using universal indicator
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Shift by increasing/decreasing concentration.

practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Shift by changing concentration of either ion.

Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration against Oxalic acid
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of hydrochloric acid by titration against Sodium Carbonate
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch/egg albumin/gum) and lyophobic sol (Al(OH)3/Ferric hydroxide)
Dialysis of prepared sols
Study the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion of different oils

Study of reaction rates (Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Clock Reaction)
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid and strong base
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
practicals.details: Thermochemistry. Hydrogen bond formation study. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in electrolyte concentration
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography
practicals.details: Chromatography. Determination of Rf values. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)

practicals.details: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds. Class XII Practical.

practicals.details: Preparation of Organic Compounds. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino)
practicals.details: Class XII Practical. Tests on organic compounds.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples
practicals.details: Detection in given foodstuffs. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Class XI)
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis. Specific ions listed for Class XI.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in drinking water
projects.details: Study causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit depending upon regional variation.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 4 (d and f Block Elements): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practicals): 10 Marks

written_test_details.question_type: Practical skill based very short answer type questions

section_b_mandatory_experiments: Quantitative analysis: Preparation of standard solution and determination of molarity using Oxalic acid or Ferrous ammonium sulphate
Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

details: Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
Students are expected to record at least five listed experiments in their practical file for visually challenged students.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 4 (d and f Block Elements): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practicals): 10 Marks

written_test_details.question_type: Practical skill based very short answer type questions

section_b_mandatory_experiments: Quantitative analysis: Preparation of standard solution and determination of molarity using Oxalic acid or Ferrous ammonium sulphate
Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

details: Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
Students are expected to record at least five listed experiments in their practical file for visually challenged students.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques -> Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of chemistry, develop conceptual foundations for real-life application, and enable representation at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, and uncertainty in measurement.
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory to calculate atomic/molecular masses.
Perform stoichiometric calculations using mole concept and percentage composition.

competency_groups.title: Structure of Atom
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in atomic structure and form connections underlying chemical phenomena through quantum mechanical understanding.
competencies: Trace the discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution from Bohr's model to Quantum Mechanical Model.

competency_groups.title: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand periodic trends, and identify patterns underlying chemical properties.
competencies: Understand the genesis of periodic classification and modern periodic law.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
competency_groups.description: Develop ability to apply bonding theories to explain molecular structure, hybridization, and intermolecular forces.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, VSEPR theory, Valence Bond Theory, and Molecular Orbital Theory.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic levels through thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes, and spontaneity.
competencies: Apply concepts of calorimetry, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium conditions.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in dynamic equilibrium and apply laws of chemical equilibrium to physical and chemical processes.
competencies: Analyze homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, ionic equilibrium, buffer solutions, and solubility equilibria.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions
competency_groups.description: Develop problem-solving skills to analyze electron transfer reactions and oxidation numbers.
competencies: Classify redox reactions in terms of electron transfer and electrode processes.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds structurally, understand reaction mechanisms, and apply purification techniques.
competencies: Apply nomenclature rules, identify isomerism, and analyze fundamental concepts of organic reaction mechanisms.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand their properties, and identify carcinogenic/toxic effects.
competencies: Differentiate between alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Develop conceptual foundations to express concentration, understand solubility, and apply colligative properties.
competencies: Calculate molar mass using colligative properties and distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent electrochemical phenomena and apply Nernst equation for cell potential calculations.
competencies: Analyze galvanic cells, electrolytic cells, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion processes.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Develop problem-solving skills to analyze factors influencing reaction rates and temperature dependence.
competencies: Apply integrated rate equations and collision theory of chemical reactions.

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in transition elements, lanthanoids, and actinoids regarding electronic configuration and properties.
competencies: Correlate position in periodic table with general properties of d-block and f-block elements.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent coordination compounds using IUPAC nomenclature and understand bonding theories.
competencies: Apply Werner's theory, identify isomerism, and analyze metal carbonyl bonding.

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
competency_groups.description: Develop ability to apply nomenclature rules, understand C-X bond nature, and predict chemical reactions.
competencies: Differentiate methods of preparation and physical properties of halo compounds.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify organic compounds based on functional groups and understand their commercial importance.
competencies: Apply nomenclature rules and distinguish structures of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.description: Develop problem-solving skills to analyze preparation methods, physical properties, and chemical reactions of carbonyl compounds.
competencies: Differentiate nomenclature and structures of aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify amines, understand diazonium salts synthesis, and apply reactions in aromatic compound synthesis.
competencies: Apply nomenclature rules and analyze chemical reactions of primary amines.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Prepare learners to contribute to frontier research areas related to biology and medicine through understanding biomolecular structures.
competencies: Identify carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours

Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
